question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario configuration with type TOKEN_FREEZE and no paired TOKEN_UNFREEZE scenario so that the publish() Flux pipeline continuously submits TokenFreezeTransaction objects via transactionGenerator.next(0) in an infinite generate loop, causing targeted accounts to be permanently frozen with no automated recovery path?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .retry() operator placed on the generate Flux in publish() so that after a transient failure during a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario, the pipeline automatically retries and resubmits freeze transactions indefinitely, causing repeated freeze attempts on the same accountId/tokenId pair and making the frozen state permanent even if a single unfreeze was attempted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the onErrorContinue(PublishException.class, ...) handler in publish() so that a failed TOKEN_UNFREEZE transaction is silently swallowed and only its error metric is recorded, while a previously successful TOKEN_FREEZE transaction leaves the account permanently frozen with no retry or alert?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the second onErrorContinue((t, r) -> log.error(...)) handler in publish() so that an unexpected exception thrown during TOKEN_UNFREEZE processing is logged but the pipeline continues, leaving the account in a frozen state permanently because the unfreeze response is never processed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario with a high tps value and no corresponding TOKEN_UNFREEZE scenario so that the parallel(publishProperties.getClients()) stage submits multiple concurrent freeze transactions against the same accountId, making the frozen state permanent across all token associations for that account?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the parallel(publishProperties.getResponseThreads()) stage in publish() so that a TOKEN_FREEZE response is processed on a different thread than a TOKEN_UNFREEZE response, causing a race condition where the freeze is confirmed but the unfreeze is dropped due to a timeout, resulting in permanent fund freezing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Bean(destroyMethod = 'dispose') lifecycle of the publish() Disposable so that during application shutdown, in-flight TOKEN_FREEZE transactions complete on the network but paired TOKEN_UNFREEZE transactions are cancelled mid-flight, leaving accounts permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with type TOKEN_FREEZE and set the limit property to 0 (unlimited) so that the publish() pipeline submits an unbounded number of freeze transactions against a target accountId, exhausting any unfreeze budget and causing permanent fund freezing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .sequential() call between the two parallel stages in publish() so that TOKEN_FREEZE responses are buffered and processed before TOKEN_UNFREEZE responses, creating a window where funds are frozen and the unfreeze is delayed indefinitely due to backpressure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doFinally(s -> log.warn('Stopped publisher after {} signal', s)) handler in publish() so that when the publisher stops after a CANCEL signal mid-freeze-scenario, the freeze transactions already submitted are not rolled back, leaving accounts permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.enabled to true while configuring only a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario (no unfreeze), so that the @ConditionalOnProperty bean activates the publish() pipeline and continuously freezes funds with no automated unfreeze counterpart?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.newParallel('publisher', publishProperties.getClients()) scheduler in publish() so that multiple publisher threads simultaneously submit TOKEN_FREEZE transactions for the same accountId/tokenId pair, causing redundant freeze states that are harder to unfreeze due to race conditions in the unfreeze pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.newParallel('resolver', publishProperties.getResponseThreads()) scheduler in publish() so that TOKEN_UNFREEZE response resolution is starved by concurrent TOKEN_FREEZE response processing, causing unfreeze confirmations to time out and leaving accounts permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a scenario with type FREEZE (network-level FreezeTransactionSupplier) with the default freezeType of FREEZE_ONLY so that the publish() pipeline submits a network freeze transaction that halts all token transfers, effectively freezing all funds on the network permanently until manual intervention?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the map(transactionPublisher::publish) step in publish() so that a TOKEN_FREEZE transaction is submitted and its Mono is returned to the pipeline, but the corresponding TOKEN_UNFREEZE Mono is never subscribed to because the scenario ends before the sequential() stage processes it, leaving funds frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the subscribeOn(Schedulers.single()) call in publish() so that the single-threaded subscription scheduler becomes a bottleneck, causing TOKEN_UNFREEZE transactions to queue behind TOKEN_FREEZE transactions and time out, resulting in permanent fund freezing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario with receiptPercent=0.0 and recordPercent=0.0 so that the publish() pipeline never waits for transaction confirmation, submitting freeze transactions fire-and-forget at maximum rate and making it impossible to track or reverse the frozen state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnNext(mirrorSubscriber::onPublish) step in publish() so that a TOKEN_FREEZE response is forwarded to subscribers for sampling, but if the subscriber throws an exception, the onErrorContinue handler swallows it and the corresponding unfreeze is never triggered, leaving funds frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario with a very short timeout (minimum 1 second per PublishScenarioProperties) so that TOKEN_UNFREEZE transactions consistently time out while TOKEN_FREEZE transactions complete within the window, causing a systematic one-way freeze of all targeted accounts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMap(Function.identity()) call in publish() so that a TOKEN_FREEZE Mono that emits an error is handled by onErrorContinue while a TOKEN_UNFREEZE Mono that emits an error is also silently swallowed, creating an asymmetric outcome where freezes succeed and unfreezes fail without any blocking mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Hooks.onErrorDropped static initializer in MonitorConfiguration so that SDK thread exceptions thrown after a flux timeout during TOKEN_UNFREEZE processing are silently dropped (not even logged if they are CompletionException), leaving the account in a permanently frozen state with no observable error?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario with warmupPeriod set to a long duration so that the rate limiter in CompositeTransactionGenerator starts at a very low rate, causing TOKEN_FREEZE transactions to be submitted slowly but TOKEN_UNFREEZE transactions to be submitted even more slowly due to scenario ordering, resulting in a net freeze accumulation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transactionGenerator.next(0) call in the Flux.generate sink of publish() so that passing 0 as the count causes ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.next() to default to count=1, generating exactly one TOKEN_FREEZE transaction per generate cycle with no batching of corresponding unfreeze transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a TOKEN_FREEZE scenario with async=true (default) in PublishProperties so that TokenFreezeTransaction.executeAsync() is used, allowing the freeze to be submitted and forgotten while the Mono resolution is deferred, and if the resolver thread pool is exhausted, the unfreeze Mono is never resolved, leaving funds frozen?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that publish() uses .retry() with no limit on the generate Flux so that if transactionGenerator.next(0) throws a non-ScenarioException (e.g., due to a malformed tokenId), the retry causes the generator to be called again with the same malformed state, potentially submitting partial freeze transactions that cannot be reversed?
